mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in over 65% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment rises during economic expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansions in retail sales and housing starts indicate robust consumer demand and construction activity, boosting corporate revenue forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 55% of cases, or interaction term IC is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which improves profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns during late-stage economic expansions.
failure_condition: Signal reverses to negative returns when industrial production rises above 105 index level, or Rank IC drops below zero in late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation erodes real corporate profits and forces monetary tightening, which increases discount rates and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns when inflation exceeds target thresholds.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 60% of high-inflation instances, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm a strengthening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding trend levels indicates broad-based economic expansion that lifts aggregate corporate earnings, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in over 55% of above-trend GDP quarters, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising federal funds rates signals stagflationary pressures that compress profit margins and increase financing costs, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in over 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term fails to predict return direction during tightening phases.
